LAW AND MOTION 301, PETITIONER CHAR ANN SCOTT WRIT OF MANDATE WAS ARGUED AND TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION ON 02/28/05. RULING: PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE IS DENIED; RESPONDENT SHALL RECOVER COSTS IN THE AMOUNT OF $295.50 WHICH ARE TO BE PAID BY PETITIONER TO THE DMV; THE STAY OF SUSPENSION OF PETITIONER'S LICENSE WILL BE DISSOLVED BUT REIMPOSITION OF THE SUSPENSION WILL BE EFFECTIVE UPON WRITTEN NOTICE BY DMV. JUDGE: JAMES L. WARREN; REPORTER: CHRISTINA PAXTON, CSR 1558

THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PETITIONER SCOTT, CHAR ANN TAKE NOTHING FROM RESPONDENT GUTIERREZ, CHON INTERIM DIRECTOR OF THE DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES CONLEY, MS. HEARING OFFICER OF THE DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES, AN AGENCY OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA ON THE COMPLAINT, SAID RESPONDENT TO HAVE JUDGMENT THEREON FOR $292.50 COSTS, TOTAL JUDGMENT $292.50, PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE IS DENIED. THE STAY OF SUSPENSION OF PETITIONER'S LICENSE WILL BE DISSOLVED. SEE SCANNED DOCUMENT
